title: 英國iNews：內政大臣正打算讓主權移交後出生的港人取得公民身份

replies.msg:
Priti Patel sets up China clash as she prepares to extend offer of UK citizenship to Hong Kong residents born after handover

Up to 2.9m Hong Kong residents with BNO status will be eligible to settling in the UK and claim British citizenship under a scheme whose details will be published after the summer.


英內政大臣準備賦予英國公民身份給香港主權移交後出生的香港人時，可能引起中英衝突。

根據計劃，將有多達290萬俱有BNO身份的香港居民有資格在英國定居併申領英國國籍，該計劃的詳細信息將在夏季之後公佈。

https://inews.co.uk/news/politics/priti-patel-china-clash-hong-kong-556618
雙非??
10月

quote.msg: 雙非??

replies.msg:
應該講返舊料：BNO承繼權
97後出生，父母是但一方係BNO

平權一部分黎

Priti Patel is risking a new row with China after promising to open the door to millions of young Hong Kong residents who could be given the right to settle in the UK despite not being British nationals.

The Home Secretary has asked officials to examine possible routes for Hongkongers who were born after 1997 – which includes many of the most vocal pro-democracy activists – to come to Britain on similar terms to the offer already extended to those who claimed British national (overseas) status before the territory was handed back to China.

Priti Patel承諾向數以百萬年輕的香港人敞開大門，儘管他們不是英國國民，這些年輕人可能被賦予在英國定居的權利，但她可能會因此與中國發生新的衝突。

內政大臣已要求官員們為1997年以後出生的香港人（包括許多在民主運動上付出的人士）探討一切可能的途徑，使他們能以跟BNO 5+1計劃相類似的方式來到英國。
其實單新聞都係標題黨嚟，內文都無話直接畀citizenship

你未搞清楚British Nationals同Citizen既分別
Nationals係大英祖國子民, 包括本土同海外屬地居民
Citizen特指英國本土公民, 指有權於英倫三島本土上居留同享有國民福利既人仕

BNO係British Nationals (British Nationals (Overseas))
所以BNO係英籍無錯, 但唔係英國公民British Citizenship
而家under 5+1, 你想由BNO呢隻英籍轉去另一隻英籍British Citizenship

s4要求如下，簡單講就係5+1

4 Acquisition by registration: [F38British overseas territories citizens] etc.
(1)This section applies to any person who is a [F39British overseas territories citizen], [F40a British National (Overseas),] a British Overseas citizen, a British subject under this Act or a British protected person.
(2)A person to whom this section applies shall be entitled, on an application for his registration as a British citizen, to be registered as such a citizen if the following requirements are satisfied in the case of that person, namely—
(a)subject to subsection (3), that he was in the United Kingdom at the beginning of the period of five years ending with the date of the application and that the number of days on which he was absent from the United Kingdom in that period does not exceed 450; and
(b)that the number of days on which he was absent from the United Kingdom in the period of twelve months so ending does not exceed 90; and
(c)that he was not at any time in the period of twelve months so ending subject under the immigration laws to any restriction on the period for which he might remain in the United Kingdom; and
(d)that he was not at any time in the period of five years so ending in the United Kingdom in breach of the immigration laws.
(3)So much of subsection (2)(a) as requires the person in question to have been in the United Kingdom at the beginning of the period there mentioned shall not apply in relation to a person who was settled in the United Kingdom immediately before commencement.
